text: She felt very _____ when she got the promotion.
I have to meet a strict _____ on Friday for this report.
Before flying, you have to check in your _____ at the airport.
I _____ with my coworkers — we have a great relationship.
If you don't know a word, _____ in the dictionary.
He's not coming to the party — he's feeling a bit _____.
_____, I think we should postpone the meeting.
She quit her job because the _____ was too low.
After the long hike, my legs were really _____.
We need to _____ our plans because of the rain.
I haven't seen Marco in years — running into him was completely _____.
The traffic was terrible, so I _____ late for the interview.
I wasn't sure about the new menu, but I decided to _____.
The company has a big _____ next month — they expect high sales.
He's a really _____ person — always thinking of others.
If you're not feeling well, you should see a _____.
I _____ in this company for five years.
If it _____ tomorrow, we will cancel the picnic.
She is _____ student in the class.
You _____ smoke in this building. It's prohibited.
I _____ to Paris next week — the tickets are already booked.
When the phone rang, I _____ dinner.
Have you _____ been to Japan?
She _____ play tennis when she was younger, but not anymore.
There isn't _____ milk left in the fridge.
Tokyo is _____ Madrid.
I _____ better study tonight; the exam is tomorrow.
The book _____ I'm reading is excellent.
We _____ already eaten when they arrived.
_____ the heavy rain, the match continued.
I'm not sure, but it _____ rain later.
If I _____ you, I would take that job.

Read: "Marta moved to Madrid three years ago for a new job. At first, she felt lonely because all her family lived in Argentina. But over time, she made new friends, joined a hiking club, and now she says Madrid feels like home. She still misses her family, but she visits them every December."

Why did Marta initially feel lonely?

Read: "The new café on Oak Street opens at 7 a.m. and closes at 9 p.m. on weekdays. On weekends, the hours are slightly different — they open at 8 a.m. and stay open until 11 p.m. They are closed on Mondays."

If you want to visit the café on Monday, what should you do?

Read: "Daniel had been preparing for his marathon for six months. He trained four times a week, watched his diet, and got plenty of rest. On race day, he didn't break any records, but he finished — and that was what mattered most to him."

What was most important to Daniel about the marathon?

Read: "In my opinion, working from home has both advantages and disadvantages. On one hand, you save time on commuting and can focus better. On the other hand, it's easy to feel disconnected from your team. I prefer a hybrid arrangement: two days at the office and three at home."

What does the speaker prefer?
options: A mix of office and home

text:
Read: "Sara had never been on a plane before. She was nervous during takeoff, but as soon as she saw the clouds from the window, she relaxed. By the end of the flight, she was already wondering when she could travel again."

How did Sara feel by the end of the flight?

Read: "Office buildings often have rules about noise. Employees should keep their phones on silent mode and avoid loud conversations near workstations. If you need to take a long call, please use one of the meeting rooms on the second floor."

Where should you go for a long phone call?
options: Meeting rooms on the second floor

text:
Read: "Carla learned to cook from her grandmother. Every Sunday, they would prepare a traditional Italian meal together: pasta, fresh sauce, and homemade bread. Now that her grandmother is gone, Carla cooks the same meal every Sunday to remember her."

Why does Carla cook the same meal every Sunday now?
options: It's the easiest meal to prepare

text:
Read: "The company offers three types of training programs: technical (for engineers), leadership (for managers), and language (for everyone). Employees can take up to two programs per year. The deadline to apply is the end of January."

Who can take the language training?
